entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: Arachis hypogaea L., the crop studied for drought tolerance

entities.name: INTERMITTENT DROUGHT
entities.description: A stress condition that varies in intensity and limits groundnut yields

entities.name: POD YIELD
entities.description: The weight of pods produced, which is negatively affected by drought stress

entities.name: ROOT DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The growth and structural development of the root system

entities.name: WATER UPTAKE
entities.description: The amount of water absorbed by the plant from the soil

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency of water use during transpiration

entities.name: YIELD COMPONENTS
entities.description: The various physiological and morphological factors that contribute to overall pod yield

entities.name: WELL WATERED
entities.description: Control condition where plants receive sufficient water

entities.name: MILD STRESS
entities.description: Low intensity intermittent drought stress denoted as DS-1

entities.name: MEDIUM STRESS
entities.description: Moderate intensity intermittent drought stress denoted as DS-2

entities.name: SEVERE STRESS
entities.description: High intensity intermittent drought stress denoted as DS-3

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DENSITY
entities.description: A measure of the length of roots per unit volume of soil

entities.name: WATER EXTRACTION
entities.description: The process of removing water from the soil profile

entities.name: RELATIVE HARVEST INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of the harvest index under stress to the harvest index under well-watered conditions

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a genotype to maintain productivity under stress conditions

entities.name: PLANT LEAF WEIGHT
entities.description: The total weight of the plant's leaves

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the plant's leaves

relationships.description: did not discriminate tolerant from sensitive lines
weakly significant relationship under water stress
closely related in all three intermittent stresses
negatively related under medium and severe stresses
lower leaf area may use water more sparingly
lower leaf area has less damaging consequences for
